summary: Tested expense account allocation feature (iteration 52). All 15 backend tests passed. Frontend UI verified: expense modal shows funding source selector, current accounts tab shows allocated_to_expenses and balance_available fields, admin can see funding source selector on approved expenses when accounts exist.

issues: Console warning: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <select>' - hydration error from React wrapper elements around select options

action_items: Minor: Fix React hydration warning for select elements in AchatsTab.jsx (span wrapper around options)
critical_code_review_comments: Backend allocation logic is solid - creates/removes repayments correctly
Frontend funding source selector only appears when availableAccounts.length > 0 (correct behavior)
The funded_affects_ca field is stored but not yet used for reporting (as documented)
seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Compte_UI_Allocation account (100,000 F) for UI testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Expense account allocation feature is fully functional. Backend tests cover: create/update/delete expense with funding, allocate/unallocate endpoints, balance integrity, error handling. Frontend shows funding source selector in expense modal and on approved expenses (admin only). Current accounts tab displays allocated_to_expenses and balance_available fields.

backend: ExpenseCreate/ExpenseUpdate with funded_by_account_id, funded_by_account_name, funded_affects_ca fields
POST /api/expenses with funded_by_account_id → auto-creates allocation repayment
PUT /api/expenses/{id} with changed funded_by_account_id → removes prior allocation, creates new
Balance integrity: allocating expense decreases balance_available, deleting restores it
frontend: Manager expense modal shows 'Payé depuis (source de financement)' selector with accounts
Checkbox 'Cette dépense est quand même déduite du CA journalier' appears when account selected
Admin Achats tab shows funding source selector on approved expenses
Current accounts tab shows 'Disponible : X F' for each account
